label: Grammar- and schema-constrained decoding

node.label: Grammar- and schema-constrained decoding
node.definition: Grammar-constrained decoding masks or prunes next tokens so every generated prefix remains extendable to a valid formal-language output.
node.why_it_matters: It can guarantee parseable syntax for tool calls, JSON, SQL, code fragments, and other machine-consumed outputs.
limitations: It guarantees form rather than truth and adds parser, automaton, and tokenizer-integration overhead.

dependencies.rationale: Grammar- and schema-constrained decoding is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Autoregressive decoding.
Grammar- and schema-constrained decoding uses Schemas, grammars & formal output languages as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Grammar- and schema-constrained decoding is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Causal, masked & structured masking.
Grammar- and schema-constrained decoding is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
